growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Dey et al. Density-dependent feedback and higher-order interactions enable coexistence in phage-bacteria community dynamics. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag041. [PMC13077301]
growth_records.snippet: We measured the growth rate r through doubling time experiments, finding that CBA strains divided every 4.2-5.8 h and PSA strains divided every 3.6-3.8 h (Fig. 2c).
growth_records.notes: Doubling times reported directly in text for the pooled group of 3 Cellulophaga baltica (CBA) strains, measured in phage-free control flasks (200 mL PC medium in 2 L flasks, ~2x10^9 initial cells, sampled by OD600/CFU every 35 min for 15h45m; strains routinely cultured at room temperature per Methods 2.1, exact numeric temperature not stated so left null). mu = ln2/doubling_time: for td=4.2h, mu=0.693/4.2=0.165 h^-1; for td=5.8h, mu=0.693/5.8=0.119 h^-1 -> range ~0.12-0.165 h^-1. Range is aggregated across the 3 CBA strains, not resolved per individual strain in the text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, oxygen]
